title: Past-only stint trend features from online quadratic fits
summary: Motorsport simulation papers model clean-lap tyre wear with stint-wise polynomials and residual variation; your current features use raw deltas but not the evolving slope/curvature of the current stint.
rationale: Motorsport simulation papers model clean-lap tyre wear with stint-wise polynomials and residual variation; your current features use raw deltas but not the evolving slope/curvature of the current stint. An online fit can expose when pace loss is accelerating just before a stop.
implementation_hint: Within each Race-Year/Driver/Stint, sort by lap and fit a past-only quadratic or rolling linear model of `LapTime (s)` or `LapTime_Delta` vs `TyreLife`; emit current residual, slope, curvature, residual z-score, and change-in-slope. Exclude current/next lap from the fit.
expected_effect: Should improve separation for borderline pit calls where absolute tyre age is similar but degradation trajectory differs.
risk: Easy to leak if the fit uses future laps or if pit-in/out laps distort the curve; validate with strict grouped temporal splits.
